questions.question: The Indian Academy of Paediatrics (IAP) has started a program called ‘TB-free air for every child’ in which state?
questions.explanation: The Indian Academy of Paediatrics (IAP) has started the ‘TB-free air for every child’ in the state of Kerala. The IAP will train its 2500 paediatricians in the treatment protocol of Tuberculosis and they will maintain a uniform and standardised procedure for the treatment.The drive will ensure to that all the children of the state will be free of TB, ahead of the target year of 2025. They will also conduct campaigns in schools and explain about malnutrition, ill effects of tobacco usage by adults and cough etiquette.

questions.question: In which city, the Union Home Minister launched India’s first Cyber Crime Prevention Unit called ‘Cyber AASHVAST’?
questions.explanation: The Union Home Minister Amit Shah recently launched a cyber-crime prevention initiative of the Gujarat Police, called ‘Cyber AASHVAST’ in Gandhinagar. The project was created as a help line for the victims affected by cyber-crimes. During the launch event, the Minister also launched an e-governance initiative called ‘VISHWAS’ which is an acronym for Video Integration and State Wide Advanced Security. It helps in improving Law and Order and helps in Crime detection and investigation through video analytics.

questions.question: Which badminton player recently won the Malaysian Masters 2020 title?
questions.explanation: World No 1 Badminton player of Japan, Kento Momota defeated former world champion Viktor Axelsen in the final match of Malaysia Masters 2020 tournament and clinched the title.Kento Momota had won 11 major titles in the year 2019 and that is the highest number of titles held in a year by any badminton player. He won the 2019 BWF World Tour finals and also was awarded the BWF Best Male Player of the Year award. He is expected to represent Japan in the upcoming Tokyo Olympics. Chinese badminton player Chen Yu Fei won the women’s singles title of the tournament.

questions.question: ‘Sahyog-Kaijin’ is an annual joint exercise between the Coast Guards of India and which country?
questions.explanation: ‘Sahyog-Kaijin’ is an annual joint exercise between the Coast Guards of India and Japan. The 19th edition of the exercise is scheduled to begin in Chennai, India. Recently, the Coast Guard Ship of Japan, ‘Echigo’ arrived in Chennai on a goodwill visit, for participating in the annual joint exercise.The exercise has been organised between the Indian and the Japanese Coast Guards for 18 years since 2000. Four Indian Coast Guard ships and a vessel from the National Institute of Ocean Technology are participating in the exercise.

questions.question: When is the Army Day celebrated across India, every year?
questions.explanation: Army Day is celebrated every year on January 15, across the country to honour the contributions made by the soldiers to the country. The 72nd Army day was recently observed on January 15, 2020. On January 15, 1949, General KM Carriappa became the first Commander-in-Chief of Indian Army after Independence. This day is being commemorated every year as Army Day. The Indian Army holds Army Parade every year in the Parade ground, New Delhi. Captain Tania Shergill becomes the first female to lead an all-men contingent at the Army Day Parade.

questions.question: Which bank has recently signed an MoU with South-Central Railway zone of the Indian Railways, for doorstep collection of earnings from the railway stations?
questions.explanation: The State Bank of India and the South-Central Railway recently signed an agreement for doorstep collection of earnings from all the 585 railway stations of the zone. At present, the earnings of the smaller stations are sent through cash chest available with the Train Guards, the process being very tedious. The agreement will ensure collection of traffic earnings by the bank and it will be remitted to the Government account without any delay.

questions.question: Taal Volcano, which was seen in news recently, is situated in which country?
questions.explanation: Taal Volcano is the second most active volcano of Phillipnes, and is located on the Luzan island of the country.Recently, the Taal volcano emitted lava and ash clouds up to nine miles into the air. Several lakhs of people are being evacuated from the capital city of Philippines Manila, fearing a major and explosive volcanic eruption. Ash falls, volcanic lightning and volcanic thunderstorms were observed in the island. The Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology (PHIVOLCS) has made the alert level to four, the highest being five.

questions.question: Where is the Institute of Liver and Biliary Sciences located?
questions.explanation: The Institute of Liver and Biliary Sciences (ILBS) is an autonomous Institute established by the Government of Delhi. It is super-speciality hospital for liver and biliary diseases located at New Delhi. It has also been given the status of University by the University Grants Commission.Recently, the President of India, Ram Nath Kovind, attended and addressed the tenth foundation day of the Institute of Liver and Biliary Sciences (ILBS) in New Delhi. He suggested to establish liver transplant programs in more public hospitals across the country and to spread awareness about organ donation.

questions.question: The second Tejas Express, which was recently flagged off, connects Mumbai with which city?
questions.explanation: The second Tejas Express in the Mumbai- Ahmedabad route was recently flagged off from Ahmedabad by the Chief Minister of Gujarat, Vijay Rupani. The commercial run is expected to start on January 18 and it will run in the route for all days of the week except Thursday. Tejas Express is a semi-high speed and fully air-conditioned train, which is run by the wholly owned subsidiary of Indian Railways, Indian Railway Catering and Tourism Corporation (IRCTC). The first Tejas Express between New Delhi and Lucknow was inaugurated in October 2019. For Tejas Express, IRCTC follows a dynamic pricing scheme and it pays compensation to the customers on train delays.

questions.question: As per the agreement recently signed to end the 23-year old Bru-Reang refugee crisis, the Bru tribals can permanently settle in which state?
questions.explanation: The Government of India, Governments of Tripura and Mizoram and Bru-Reang representatives signed the historic agreement in New Delhi recently, which will end the 23-year old Bru-Reang refugee crisis. Under the new agreement, around 34,000 Bru refugees, who were displaced from Mizoram will be permanently settled in Tripura and would be given aid from the Centre to assist their rehabilitation and development, through a fund of Rs 600 crores. Each displaced family will also be given residential plots by the Tripura Government.
